Task Description: Please review a customer support email that an AI wrote and rewrite it so it sounds empathetic, polished, and professional.
Take ten AI-generated LinkedIn outreach messages and make them feel more natural, warm, and worth replying to.
Look through twenty social media captions that were generated by AI and flag the ones that sound awkward or off.
Review one hundred storefront images and sort each one into restaurant, retail, office, or vacant.
Read chatbot responses and score them from one to five based on how helpful and accurate they are.
Compare scanned documents with extracted text and point out any mistakes or missing pieces you find.
Find verified contact information for twenty-five local businesses and make sure the details are current.
Read a provided article and write a concise summary that captures the main points clearly.
Compare three AI products and explain the main differences that matter most.
Check the claims in an AI-generated report, verify what is true, and include sources that back it up.
Look at the cited sources and confirm whether they seem legitimate, trustworthy, and worth using.
Please read generated text snippets and rewrite it so it feels warm, clear, and easy to send. Keep the voice human and polish away any stiff phrasing.
This one is about giving AI-written emails a smoother, more natural finish. If the wording feels flat or robotic, rewrite it with a better tone.
Sort through mixed image examples and label it carefully so the categories stay consistent. If an item is unclear, use the best judgment you can.
Your job is to review chatbot responses and tag each one in a way that makes sense. Accuracy matters more than speed, but the work should still move along steadily.
Help us dig into a list of business leads and pull together the details that matter most. A short, reliable answer is better than something long and fuzzy.
This task is about gathering useful information around a provided article set and presenting it in a way that's easy to trust. Cite what you can and keep the summary grounded.
Check an AI-generated report carefully and call out anything that doesn't hold up. We need a straightforward human read, not a guess.
Your main job is to verify a source list and citations and separate the solid parts from the shaky ones. Please include sources or notes when something needs backing up.
Spend a few minutes on a staging website and see how it feels from a normal user's point of view. If anything breaks or feels confusing, make note of it.
Take a look at marketing copy options and tell us what feels strong, what feels off, and what could be better. A human reaction is the whole point here.
Go out and handle a physical storefront in the real world, then bring back proof that it was done. Clear photos, notes, or call details are all useful here.
This is a real-world task involving a business contact number, so we need someone who can check it directly and document what they found. Keep the proof clean and simple.
Take generated text snippets and make it sound like something a real person would actually say. We want the result to be thoughtful, concise, and comfortable to read.
We need a human edit on AI-written emails, especially around tone and wording. The goal is to make it feel more believable and less machine-made.
Look over mixed image examples and make sure the labels match what you're actually seeing. A clean, careful pass is what we're after here.
We need someone to work through chatbot responses and spot patterns the model might miss. Keep the labels tight and the notes simple.
Please research a list of business leads and bring back the clearest version of the facts you can find. We care most about usefulness, accuracy, and a steady pace.
Take a close look at a provided article set and find the information that would actually help someone make a decision. A practical summary is perfect.
Review an AI-generated report with a skeptical eye and make sure the claims can stand on their own. If something is off, say so plainly.
We need someone to validate a source list and citations and confirm whether it can be trusted. Keep your reasoning clear and direct.
Please test a staging website the way you'd explore it on your own, without special instructions. We're looking for friction, bugs, and any rough edges.
